label: bad_neighborhood_rescue_burst_bot
description: A sparse burst-style bot that scores LOW alone. As the store fills with similar accounts we previously flagged, memory lifts it into a flagged tier.

label: bad_neighborhood_rescue_promo_bot
description: A sparse promo bot, LOW alone. Memory recognises the behavioral family from prior scans.

label: bad_neighborhood_rescue_generic_bot
description: Generic low-effort bot, LOW alone, rescued by accumulated memory.

label: good_neighborhood_no_false_lift
description: A real viewer with a sparse-but-genuine comment. The store fills with similar accounts we previously cleared; memory must NOT escalate it.

posts.text: The bond ladder example at 14 minutes was the clearest explanation of that I have seen, finally understand why my advisor structured mine the way he did.
Came back to rewatch the sequencing-risk part before my own retirement meeting next week. Thanks for keeping it jargon-light.

label: distant_neighborhood_inert
description: A bot whose fingerprint matches nothing in the store, even though the store is full of high-suspicion accounts. The memory signal must stay at zero confidence and leave the score untouched at every store size — conservatism guard.
